bottleneck.description: Zero tensor core utilization across all 40 aten::mm kernels. smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0.0 on every instance. Kernel2 is cuBLAS FP32 SGEMM — Blackwell WGMMA/Tensor Cores only activate on BF16/FP16/TF32 inputs.

transformation.description: Cast all nn.Linear weights and activations to torch.bfloat16 to force cuBLAS dispatch to sm90_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16 / Blackwell WGMMA path.
transformation.fx_graph_change: Inject aten._to_copy.default(x, dtype=torch.bfloat16) upstream of every aten::mm node. Alternatively: model.to(torch.bfloat16) before torch.compile().

estimated_impact.latency: 5–15x speedup on all mm kernels; total forward pass ~3.5ms → ~0.3–0.7ms

bottleneck.description: Wave starvation. Grid [16,2,6]x128 = 192 warps total. On Blackwell 132 SMs this is ~0.36 waves — SMs spend >90% of cycles idle waiting for the next wave.

transformation.description: Batch the 10 repeated same-shape mm calls into a single batched GEMM to expand wave count, or increase batch size to M>=2048 to fill SM waves.
transformation.fx_graph_change: Replace sequence of 10 aten::mm nodes of identical shape with aten::bmm on a [10,256,512] input tensor. At batch*M=2560 the grid expands to ~3.6 waves. Alternatively: torch.compile(mode='max-autotune') will select tile sizes that fill available SMs at M=256.

estimated_impact.latency: 3–6x on small/down-proj mm; ~1ms saved across 20 affected kernels

bottleneck.description: Unfused GEMM epilogue. Kernel2 writes matmul output to HBM; separate Triton elementwise kernel reads it back for bias-add + activation. Two kernel launches and one full intermediate tensor materialization per MLP layer. 40 redundant kernel launches total.

transformation.description: Fuse aten::mm → aten::add (bias) → activation into a single kernel with GEMM epilogue. Collapse two kernels into one, eliminating one HBM write+read per layer.
transformation.fx_graph_change: Match pattern: aten::mm → aten::add → aten::{relu,gelu,silu,tanh}. Replace with torch.nn.functional.linear (which emits aten::addmm) and ensure torch.compile inductor epilogue fusion is active. For full control, use a Triton GEMM kernel with epilogue_fn parameter.
transformation.code_example:
# Ensure nn.Linear is used (not manual mm + add) so inductor can fuse the epilogue
# torch.compile will emit triton_mm_with_epilogue when activation follows addmm

estimated_impact.throughput: Minor improvement from reduced dispatch overhead
estimated_impact.memory: Saves ~80MB HBM traffic (one output tensor read per layer x 40 layers)

bottleneck.description: tanh kernels show lowest SM throughput (1.4–1.5%) and lowest occupancy (6.8–8.2%) of all activation kernels. smsp__inst_executed.avg.per_cycle_active = 0.05 vs 0.10–0.22 for relu/gelu/silu — the SFU pipeline (__tanhf special function) serializes warp issue, stalling the scheduler. DRAM read anomaly: 531–532 units vs 2.11 for peers (likely profiling normalization artifact for high-SFU-stall kernels).

transformation.description: Replace aten::tanh with aten::gelu(approximate='tanh') which uses a degree-3 polynomial approximation via ALU instead of SFU __tanhf, avoiding special function pipeline serialization.
transformation.fx_graph_change: Pattern match aten.tanh.default nodes in FX graph. Replace with aten.gelu.default(x, approximate='tanh'). If exact tanh semantics required, write a Triton kernel using tl.math.tanh which compiles to vectorized polynomial on Blackwell.

estimated_impact.memory: Negligible change in actual HBM traffic

optimizations.notes: Assumption: 531MB DRAM read metric on tanh vs 2.11MB on peers reflects NCU normalization window distortion under high SFU stall latency, not actual 250x more HBM traffic. SM throughput evidence (1.4% vs 5–11%) is metric-independent and confirms SFU bottleneck regardless.

bottleneck.description: Suboptimal GEMM tile configuration from inductor default (non-autotuned) mode. dynamic_smem_per_block = 32.77KB allocated but L1 hit rate only 25.6% and smsp__issue_active = 23% — tile blocking is insufficient for M=256. Without autotuning, cuBLAS selects a generic tile that leaves occupancy at 16.6%.

transformation.description: Enable torch.compile max-autotune mode to benchmark GEMM tile configs for the [256,2048]x[2048,2048] shape. Expected winning config: 256x128 or 128x256 Triton tiles with BF16 Tensor Core path. Fallback without BF16: set torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32=True to enable TF32 on FP32 inputs.
transformation.fx_graph_change: No FX graph change required. Change compilation invocation: torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune', fullgraph=True). Inductor will emit a shape-specific autotuned kernel replacing the generic Kernel2.
transformation.code_example:
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True  # TF32 fallback if staying FP32
model = torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune', fullgraph=True)

estimated_impact.latency: 20–40% reduction on large mm kernels after autotuning

optimizations.notes: Impact is conditional on OPT-001 (BF16 cast) being applied first. TF32 provides ~8x theoretical throughput vs SGEMM at cost of 3 mantissa bits precision loss.

global_notes: All tensor core metrics are zero without exception — smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 on every kernel. OPT-001 (BF16 dtype) is a prerequisite for all GEMM-level throughput improvements.
Kernel2 identity: cuBLAS opaque name for FP32 SGEMM on Blackwell. Does not map to WMMA/WGMMA. Confirmed by smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0.

aten::addmm attribution confidence is LOW (name_heuristic, no NVTX range). The 40-kernel aggregation under call_index=0 is a profiler-side limitation from inductor not emitting NVTX markers in default mode.
Recommended fix order: OPT-001 → OPT-005 → OPT-002 → OPT-003 → OPT-004
